title: W-2 form
description: Practice reading and understanding the W-2 form

assessment_items.question_content:
One of the most commonly used tax forms is a W-2.

**Who generally gives you a W-2 form?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: your employer
the government
your bank

assessment_items.question_content:
One of the most commonly used tax forms is a W-2.

**Who needs a W-2 form?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: anyone who had a job last year
only people who have their own business
anyone under 18 years old

assessment_items.question_content:
One of the most commonly used tax forms is a W-2.

**What is the main use of a W-2 form?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: to report your yearly income and taxes withheld
to report the interest you earned from your bank
to list your deductions and credits for the year

assessment_items.question_content:
Information on a W-2 can be grouped into two groups: sections (*labeled a-f*) and boxes (*numbered 1-20*). 

**What information is in sections a-f?**
[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: Close‑up of a W‑2 tax form highlighting sections a-f. Included are the employer's tax ID number and address as well as the employee's name, social security number, and address.

choices.content: your job title and job description
your personal information and your employer's information
the history of your previous jobs for the last 5 years

assessment_items.question_content:
Information on a W-2 can be grouped into two groups: sections (*labeled a-f*) and boxes (*numbered 1-20*). 

**What information is in boxes 1-20?**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: Close‑up of a W‑2 tax form highlighting boxes 1–20. Includes boxes for wages, tips, and other compensation as well as paid federal, social security, Medicare, and state taxes.

choices.content: your job title and job description
your personal information and your employer's information
details about your income and any withholdings

assessment_items.question_content:
You received your W-2 and want to find out how much income tax you paid.

**Where on the W-2 form are tax withholdings?**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: a W‑2 tax form.  Boxes 1 through 20 list wages, tips, and other compensation, federal, social security, Medicare, and state taxes withheld. Sections a through f list employer's and employee's names and addresses.

choices.content: in the sections a-f
in boxes 1-20
on the backside of the form

assessment_items.question_content:
Below is information from boxes 1-6 on your W-2 form.

W-2 form | 
-|-
**1**  *Wages, tips, other compensation*: $\$48{,}000$ | **2** *Federal income tax withheld*: $\$5{,}760$ 
**3** *Social security wages*: $\$50{,}000$ | **4** *Social security tax withheld*: $\$3{,}100$ 
**5** *Medicare wages and tips*: $\$50{,}000$ | **6** *Medicare tax withheld*: $\$725$ 

**What could be a reason why the amount of wages in box 1 is $\$2{,}000$ *less* than the amount of wages in box 3?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The employer made a mistake when filling out the W2.
$\$2{,}000$  was put into a retirement account.
$\$2{,}000$  was taken out for state and local taxes.

assessment_items.question_content:
Below is information from boxes 1-6 on your W-2 form.

W-2 form | 
-|-
**1**  *Wages, tips, other compensation*: $\$30{,}000$ | **2** *Federal income tax withheld*: $\$3{,}600$ 
**3** *Social security wages*: $\$32{,}000$ | **4** *Social security tax withheld*: $\$1{,}984$ 
**5** *Medicare wages and tips*: $\$32{,}000$ | **6** *Medicare tax withheld*: $\$464$


**What could be a reason why the amount of wages in box 3 is $\$2{,}000$ *more* than the amount of wages in box 1?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The employer made a mistake when filling out the W2.
You paid  $\$2{,}000$ for health insurance.
$\$2{,}000$  was taken out for state and local taxes.

assessment_items.question_content:
Below is information from boxes 1-6 on your W-2 form.

W-2 form | 
-|-
**1**  *Wages, tips, other compensation*: $\$45{,}000$ | **2** *Federal income tax withheld*: $\$6{,}750$ 
**3** *Social security wages*: $\$47{,}500$ | **4** *Social security tax withheld*: $\$2{,}940$ 
**5** *Medicare wages and tips*: $\$47{,}500$ | **6** *Medicare tax withheld*: $\$689$


**What could be a reason why the amount of wages in box 3 is $\$2{,}500$ *more* than the amount of wages in box 1?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The employer made a mistake when filling out the W2.
$\$2{,}500$ was taken out for 401(k) and health insurance.
$\$2{,}500$ shows the amount earned in tips.
